key_insight: A short-horizon MPC can capture long-term fruit-production economics by using an RL rollout to supply terminal cost and terminal region information, then selecting the better of the MPC or RL first action.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical control strategy for greenhouse systems that preserves constraint handling while reducing the computational burden of long-horizon nonlinear MPC.
method: Use RL offline to generate a longer-term trajectory under weather uncertainty.
Embed the RL trajectory as terminal ingredients in a short-horizon nonlinear MPC problem.
Execute the first control input from whichever trajectory has the better objective value.
evidence_signal: In simulations on the large-scale GreenLight tomato greenhouse model, the proposed one-hour-horizon method matched a strong guiding policy and outperformed standalone one-hour MPC.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality and safety of the RL rollout, so unseen weather patterns or poor trajectory estimates could still affect performance.
paper_title: Improving greenhouse fruit-production control by integrating reinforcement learning into short-horizon model predictive control
